title: Shift-Aware Training Via Train-vs-Test Weighting And Prior-Shift Checks
summary: The test set has fewer drivers than train and includes testing/wet regimes, so ordinary CV may mis-rank candidates under covariate or label shift.
rationale: The test set has fewer drivers than train and includes testing/wet regimes, so ordinary CV may mis-rank candidates under covariate or label shift. Domain-adaptation work supports importance weighting under covariate shift and BBSE-style prior correction under label shift.
implementation_hint: Train a lightweight classifier to distinguish train from test rows using only features available at inference; use clipped density-ratio style weights for main-model training or for importance-weighted CV scoring. Separately, estimate whether positive-class prior shifts materially across regimes via BBSE on OOF predictions, then test a small post-hoc logit intercept correction.
expected_effect: Should reduce local-CV optimism and improve leaderboard transfer if current folds underrepresent target-like regimes.
risk: Weight estimates can be high variance and harm strong tree models; clip aggressively and compare against an unweighted baseline.
